macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum post modest 24-hour gains as crypto market stays cautious
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 1.81% to about $63,684, while Ethereum gained 1.54% to about $1,675, according to market coverage summarizing the past 24 hours. The report also noted a broader sector uptick, with DeFi TVL up 2.58% and NFT sales volume up 18.4%.

items.title: Top daily crypto movers include Velvet Unicorn by Virtuals and StablR USD
items.summary: The same market wrap highlighted sharp single-day moves among smaller assets, including Velvet Unicorn by Virtuals up 288.74% and StablR USD among the day’s notable gainers. It also flagged an outsized 24-hour TVL jump in VisionBoard Vault.

items.title: Visa outlines stablecoin settlement and AI agent verification plans
items.summary: Visa said it plans to position itself as a trust layer for independent commerce, linking $7 billion in stablecoin settlement activity with AI agent verification. The development adds to growing institutional interest in blockchain-based payments infrastructure.

items.title: Hong Kong tightens regulations on mainland investors’ cross-border fund flows
items.summary: A crypto market update reported that Hong Kong has strengthened rules affecting mainland investors and cross-border fund flows. The move is relevant to digital assets because regional capital controls can affect trading access and crypto market liquidity.

items.title: Japan approves bill to cut Bitcoin and Ethereum taxes
items.summary: The market wrap said Japan authorized a bill reducing taxes on Bitcoin and Ethereum from 55% to 20%. If implemented as described, the change would materially improve tax treatment for crypto investors in Japan.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Nothing phone and audio products coming to Best Buy for the first time
items.summary: Nothing’s smartphones and headphones will soon be sold in Best Buy stores and online, expanding the brand’s U.S. retail reach. The move signals a broader push into mainstream consumer distribution.

items.title: Microsoft patches critical Surface firmware flaw
items.summary: Microsoft issued a fix for a critical firmware vulnerability that could potentially brick Surface devices. The update is part of a broader wave of security patches affecting major tech platforms.

items.title: Oracle warns of critical PeopleSoft flaw under active exploitation
items.summary: Oracle released a security advisory for a critical PeopleSoft vulnerability that is reportedly being actively exploited. The issue affects enterprise customers and raises immediate patching concerns.

items.title: EU rejects Apple DMA exemption request, delaying European Siri rollout
items.summary: European regulators rejected Apple’s request for an exemption under the Digital Markets Act, which delays the company’s European Siri rollout. The decision adds another regulatory hurdle for Apple in the region.

items.title: Instagram adds global profile grid reorganization tools
items.summary: Instagram launched an update that lets users manually rearrange their profile grids. The change gives creators and regular users more control over how profiles are presented.

items.title: Bluesky launches Reddit-style Communities
items.summary: Bluesky is rolling out Communities, a feature designed to organize discussions around shared interests in a more forum-like format. The update is part of the platform’s continued expansion beyond a simple microblogging feed.

items.title: Xbox signals a stronger push to compete in gaming
items.summary: Recent reporting suggests Microsoft is preparing a more aggressive Xbox strategy as it tries to strengthen its position in the gaming market. The development points to renewed focus on hardware, content, and platform growth.

items.title: YouTube adds messaging features
items.summary: YouTube introduced new messaging capabilities, expanding how users can share and discuss content on the platform. The update is part of ongoing social and community feature development.

items.title: SpaceX satellite deployment deadline extended by the FCC
items.summary: The FCC granted SpaceX more time to meet a satellite deployment deadline, giving the company additional runway for its network plans. The decision affects SpaceX’s broader satellite infrastructure strategy.

items.title: Google faces liability ruling over AI Overviews in Germany
items.summary: A German court said Google can be held liable for AI Overviews, increasing legal pressure on the company’s search product. The ruling may influence how AI-generated search features are treated in Europe.

items.title: AOL dial-up service shuts down after decades
items.summary: AOL has ended its long-running dial-up internet service, marking the close of one of the web’s oldest consumer access technologies. The shutdown reflects how fully internet access has shifted to broadband and mobile networks.

items.title: DoJ approves Paramount Skydance-Warner Bros. deal
items.summary: The Justice Department approved the Paramount Skydance-Warner Bros. deal, clearing a major media consolidation move. The approval cements a significant shift in ownership control across film, television, and news assets.

items.title: Facebook, WhatsApp, and Instagram reported outages
items.summary: Users reported downtime affecting Facebook, WhatsApp, and Instagram, causing disruptions across Meta’s services. The outage underscored the scale of dependency on Meta’s platforms.

items.title: Google sues Chinese scammers using Gemini in fraud operation
items.summary: Google filed suit against a Chinese cybercrime network it says used Gemini in a large-scale scam operation. The case highlights growing misuse of platform tools in cybercrime.
